name: Rijksmuseum
notes: National museum with Dutch Golden Age masterpieces; book tickets in advance to avoid queues.

name: Van Gogh Museum
notes: World's largest Van Gogh collection; timed entry tickets essential — sell out weeks ahead.

name: Anne Frank House
notes: Historic hiding place of Anne Frank; timed tickets must be booked weeks in advance online.

name: Stedelijk Museum
notes: Modern and contemporary art museum with a striking bathtub-shaped extension.

name: Royal Palace Amsterdam
notes: 17th-century civic building turned royal palace on Dam Square; interior is lavishly decorated.

name: NEMO Science Museum
notes: Interactive science museum; rooftop terrace has great harbor views and is free to access.

name: EYE Filmmuseum
notes: Striking film museum across the IJ from Centraal Station; free ferry from the station.

name: Dutch Resistance Museum
notes: Powerful museum about WWII resistance in the Netherlands; less crowded than Anne Frank House.

name: Jewish Historical Museum
notes: Four historic synagogues housing exhibitions on Jewish history and culture in the Netherlands.

name: Moco Museum
notes: Modern and contemporary art including Banksy; popular with younger visitors, book online.

name: Foam Photography Museum
notes: Intimate photography museum in a canal house; changing exhibitions from world-class photographers.

name: Amsterdam Museum
notes: City history museum tracing Amsterdam's evolution from fishing village to global metropolis.

name: Tropenmuseum
notes: Anthropology museum in a stunning 1920s building; explores cultures from around the world.

name: Artis Royal Zoo
notes: Europe's oldest zoo (1838); includes planetarium and aquarium within the grounds.

name: Micropia
notes: World's only microbe zoo; part of Artis complex. Fascinating and unique indoor experience.

name: Hortus Botanicus Amsterdam
notes: Beautiful 17th-century botanical garden with tropical greenhouses; peaceful and uncrowded.

name: A'DAM Lookout Tower
notes: 360° views over Amsterdam from a rooftop swing; across the IJ, free ferry from Centraal.

name: Heineken Experience
notes: Interactive brewery tour in the original Heineken brewery; includes beer tastings at the end.

name: Concertgebouw
notes: World-class concert hall; free 30-min lunchtime concerts on Wednesdays at 12:30.

name: Paradiso
notes: Iconic music venue in a former church; hosts international acts and club nights.

name: Vondelpark
notes: Amsterdam's most popular park; free open-air concerts in summer at the Openluchttheater.

name: Amsterdamse Bos
notes: Huge urban forest on the southern edge of the city; great for cycling, rowing, and picnics.

name: Westerpark
notes: Park in a former industrial complex; Westergasfabriek hosts markets, festivals, and events.

name: Keukenhof
notes: World-famous tulip garden open mid-March to mid-May only; easily reached by bus from Schiphol.

name: Amsterdam Canal Cruise
notes: Covered boats offer year-round canal tours; many operators depart from near Centraal Station.

name: Begijnhof
notes: Hidden medieval courtyard in the city centre; a tranquil oasis away from the crowds.

name: Jordaan Neighbourhood Walk
notes: Picturesque 17th-century neighbourhood with narrow canals, boutiques, cafés, and galleries.

name: De 9 Straatjes (Nine Streets)
notes: Grid of charming shopping streets with independent boutiques, vintage stores, and cafés.

name: De Pijp Neighbourhood
notes: Vibrant multicultural neighbourhood; hub of the Albert Cuyp Market and great restaurant scene.

name: Bloemenmarkt (Flower Market)
notes: The world's only floating flower market on the Singel canal; more tourist-oriented now but colourful.

name: Albert Cuyp Market
notes: Europe's largest outdoor daily market; great for street food, fresh produce, and cheap goods.

name: Foodhallen
notes: Indoor food hall in a converted tram depot in Oud-West; 21 food stalls and craft beer.

name: Brouwerij 't IJ
notes: Craft brewery inside a historic windmill; tours available, great terrace on sunny days.

name: Wynand Fockink
notes: Historic jenever (Dutch gin) tasting room since 1679; tiny but atmospheric, no cocktails.

name: Noordermarkt
notes: Monday antiques market and Saturday organic farmers market in the Jordaan; very local feel.

name: Café de Jaren
notes: Grand café with large canalside terrace; great for breakfast, lunch, or evening drinks.

name: Café 't Smalle
notes: Classic brown café in the Jordaan, dating to 1786; lovely waterside terrace in summer.

name: Pllek
notes: Trendy bar/restaurant in Amsterdam Noord with an artificial beach on the IJ; great views.

name: Restaurant De Kas
notes: Farm-to-table restaurant in a stunning 1926 greenhouse; reserve well in advance.

name: Hannekes Boom
notes: Laid-back waterfront bar with big terrace near Centraal Station; popular with locals.

name: Lot Sixty One Coffee Roasters
notes: Top-notch specialty coffee roaster in the Jordaan; great espresso and filter options.

name: Buffet van Odette
notes: Charming lunch café in the canal belt; known for hearty soups, salads, and homemade cakes.

name: Westerkerk
notes: 17th-century church with the tallest tower in Amsterdam; tower tours offer great views.

name: Portuguese Synagogue
notes: Magnificent 17th-century synagogue still lit by candles; a hidden gem near the Jewish Quarter.

name: Museum Van Loon
notes: Elegant 17th-century canal house museum showing how Amsterdam's elite lived; lovely garden.

name: De Drie Fleschjes
notes: Classic brown café proeflokaal (tasting room) since 1650; traditional jenever and liqueurs.

name: Restaurant Rijsel
notes: Popular bistro in a former school gym serving French-Flemish cuisine; no reservations, arrive early.

name: Poffertjes van Wonderen
notes: Classic Dutch mini-pancakes (poffertjes) with butter and powdered sugar; a must-try street food.

name: Lanskroon Bakery (Stroopwafels)
notes: Bakery famed for handmade stroopwafels and pastries; try a fresh warm stroopwafel.

name: NXT Museum
notes: Immersive new media art museum in Amsterdam Noord; features large-scale digital installations.

name: Westergasfabriek
notes: 19th-century gas factory converted into a cultural complex; hosts markets, festivals, and concerts.

name: Blijburg aan Zee
notes: Urban beach on the artificial IJburg island; popular summer spot with food stalls and live music.

name: Cobra Museum of Modern Art
notes: Modern art museum in Amstelveen, 10 min from Schiphol by tram; CoBrA movement and temporary exhibitions.

name: Royal FloraHolland Aalsmeer
notes: World's largest flower auction, 5 km from Schiphol; observation gallery open early mornings weekdays only.

name: Schiphol Airport Panorama Terrace
notes: Free viewing terrace between terminals 2 and 3 at Schiphol; great for watching planes take off.

name: Rijksmuseum at Schiphol
notes: Small free museum inside Schiphol airport (airside, between gates E and F); original Dutch masterpieces.

name: Haarlem
notes: Beautiful historic city 15 min by train from Schiphol or Amsterdam; less crowded than Amsterdam.

name: Teylers Museum
notes: Netherlands' oldest museum in an exquisite 18th-century building; natural history and art collections.

name: Amsterdam Centraal Station
notes: Stunning neo-Renaissance station from 1889; the IJ waterfront behind it offers great views and ferries.

name: Amsterdam Light Festival (winter)
notes: Annual canal light art festival running late November to late January; best viewed by boat or on foot.

name: Sarphatipark
notes: Small Victorian park in the heart of De Pijp; lovely for a picnic or afternoon break.

name: Oosterpark
notes: Green park in East Amsterdam; home to the Scream Monument and a popular local picnic spot.

name: NDSM Wharf
notes: Gritty creative hub in an old shipyard; street art, markets, bars, and cultural events.

name: Canal Bike / City Bike Rental
notes: Cycling is the best way to explore Amsterdam; many rental shops near Centraal Station.

name: Stedelijk Bureau Exhibitions (W139)
notes: Non-commercial contemporary art space in the Red Light District; free entry, experimental shows.

name: Dutch National Opera & Ballet
notes: World-class opera and ballet in the Stopera building on the Amstel; book well in advance.

name: Prinsengracht Canal Walk
notes: The most picturesque canal; walk from Westerkerk south toward the Seven Bridges for classic views.

name: Gassan Diamonds
notes: Free guided diamond-cutting tours in a historic 1879 building; no purchase pressure.

name: Red Light District (De Wallen)
notes: Amsterdam's oldest medieval quarter; interesting to walk in daylight for history and architecture.

name: Bob's Juice Bar
notes: Quick fresh juices and smoothies in the centre; good stop near the Begijnhof.

name: IJ-Kantine
notes: Canteen-style restaurant on the IJ waterfront in Amsterdam Noord; reached by free ferry.
